title: hephaestus-9up-coverage
prompt:
Match the exact painterly cinematic concept-art style, palette, color, and rendering of the reference image — cool electric blue and violet shadow, warm gold automata, orange spark-light, volumetric haze, visible brushwork. Reproduce its look precisely; introduce no style not present in the reference.

Render ONE finished image: a clean 3x3 grid of nine cinematic storyboard panels, thin black borders, narrow gutters. At the TOP-LEFT of each panel print a small number and a short SHOT-TYPE caption in white (e.g. '1. WIDE ESTABLISHER'). This is editorial coverage of a SINGLE continuous moment in one place — same character, same gold automata, same world and look across all nine.

SUBTEXT to carry in every panel: Hephaestus, the cast-out smith-god, inspecting one of the endless identical golden automata he builds — weary tenderness edged with unease, the unspoken question of whether the thing looks back.

180-DEGREE RULE across all panels: the axis of action runs between Hephaestus (screen LEFT, facing right) and the automaton he inspects (screen RIGHT, facing left); the camera never crosses this line, so in the close-ups Hephaestus looks screen-right and the automaton looks screen-left — they read as looking at each other.

Panels:
1. WIDE ESTABLISHER — the cavernous forge, the long line of gold automata receding to a vanishing point; Hephaestus small at his station, lower left.
2. MEDIUM — Hephaestus lifting a finished automaton off the line.
3. CLOSE UP — Hephaestus's weary, lined face, looking screen-right.
4. HANDS ON AUTOMATON — insert: his hands cradling the automaton's gold face and shoulder.
5. CLOSE UP — the automaton's serene gold face, looking screen-left, eyes meeting his.
6. LOW ANGLE — the gold automata looming overhead against the haze.
7. SPARK DETAIL — a welder arm sparking on a gold seam.
8. OVER THE SHOULDER — from behind the automaton, the receding army of identical others with tiny Hephaestus among them.
9. FINAL WIDE — Hephaestus alone among the silent ranks, a vast shadow falling across them.

One continuous workshop, one light direction (cool key from above, warm spark-light from the welders), consistent across all nine panels.
